market.advice: Risk-off rotation, not risk-off collapse. SPY (742) sits just under its 50-day with a rising-but-still-calm VIX (17.8) and one distribution day; breadth is a healthy 59% because leadership rotated rather than broke. Money is leaving stretched mega-cap tech and semis (NVDA, AMD, MU, ORCL, TSLA all well off highs) and flooding into financials, REITs, industrials/transports, healthcare and — with the U.S.-Iran conflict now in its tenth night of strikes and Strait of Hormuz shipping disrupted — energy. Ceasefire headlines are nudging futures up, but oil is the swing factor. Trade selectively into the open: favor cheap, un-extended value/defensive breakouts sitting AT new highs (A+ only), keep size smaller, and do NOT chase the overbought momentum names into their prints.

picks.name: Werner Enterprises, Inc.
picks.thesis: The single cleanest breakout on the board and top score in the scan — Werner is printing fresh 52-week highs on a textbook VCP as freight rates firm and money rotates into transports. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY (16 buy / 1 sell), a full confirmation of the technicals.
strengths: Top score 95, VCP + RS at new highs (0% off high)
Revenue +13.6% as the trucking cycle turns up
risks: Earnings in ~8 days — gap risk into the print

picks.name: Federal Realty Investment Trust
picks.thesis: A blue-chip shopping-center REIT and rare Dividend King breaking to new highs as rates ease and income rotation accelerates. RSI 54 leaves plenty of room to run, and TV agrees with a BUY (15/3).
strengths: Score 94.5, VCP at highs with RSI only 54 (not extended)
risks: REIT rally unwinds if long yields snap back up
watch: Hold above the breakout; volume on the push through highs

picks.name: Kimco Realty Corporation
picks.thesis: Open-air shopping-center REIT sitting right at new highs (-0.3%) with a tight VCP and RSI 53 — the un-extended income breakout the current tape rewards. TV confirms BUY (15/2).
strengths: At highs, VCP, RSI 53 — room to run
Rotation into REITs as rates ease

picks.name: Simon Property Group, Inc.
picks.thesis: Mall REIT bellwether at new highs (-0.2%) with revenue +19% and a VCP into the breakout. RSI 51 is dead-neutral — no chase required. TV confirms BUY (15/3).

picks.name: DaVita Inc.
picks.thesis: Dialysis leader breaking out just 1% off highs with EPS +43% and a cheap 13.6x multiple — defensive-growth exactly where money is rotating. TV confirms BUY (13/3).
strengths: EPS +43%, ~13.6x forward — cheap for the growth

picks.name: CVS Health Corporation
picks.thesis: A genuine turnaround breakout — CVS is AT new highs (0% off) with +42% three-month momentum, EPS +63% and a 12.8x multiple. Cheap, defensive and confirmed by TV BUY (14/3).
strengths: At highs, RS at high, VCP

picks.name: Principal Financial Group, Inc.
picks.thesis: Insurer/asset manager breaking out near highs with a 10.9x multiple and enormous EPS normalization (+819% off a depressed base). VCP + RS at high, and TV confirms BUY (11/5).
strengths: Score 93.9, VCP + RS at high, cheap 10.9x

picks.name: Bank of America Corporation
picks.thesis: Money-center bank breaking to new highs on the financials rotation with revenue +21%, EPS +36% and an 11.5x multiple. Earnings already out (~85 days to next), so no imminent gap risk. TV confirms BUY (14/3).
strengths: Rev +21%, EPS +36%; no near-term earnings risk
risks: Leveraged to the yield curve / credit cycle

picks.name: U.S. Bancorp
picks.thesis: Super-regional bank at new highs, VCP + RS at high, cheap at 10.9x with revenue +10% and EPS +22%. Earnings ~86 days out means a clean runway. TV confirms BUY (13/4).

picks.name: The Travelers Companies, Inc.
picks.thesis: P&C insurer at fresh highs with EPS +57% and a 12.5x multiple — and the strongest TV rating in the group (STRONG BUY, 17/2). RSI 72 is hot, so buy strength but respect the stop.
watch: Don't chase a gap; buy pullbacks to the pivot

picks.name: EOG Resources, Inc.
picks.thesis: Best-in-class E&P with the timeliest macro tailwind on the board — the U.S.-Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption are bidding oil. EOG is a VCP just 4% off highs, cheap at 9.6x, rev +15%. TV confirms STRONG BUY (16/1).
risks: A sudden Iran ceasefire could unwind the oil bid fast

picks.name: ONEOK, Inc.
picks.thesis: Midstream (fee-based, less oil-price-sensitive) breaking out near highs with rev +19.6% and a 15x multiple — an energy exposure with a fat dividend and steadier cash flow than pure E&P. TV confirms BUY (15/2).
strengths: VCP near highs, rev +19.6%, income component
Fee-based cash flow buffers oil volatility

picks.name: Altria Group, Inc.
picks.thesis: Deep-defensive at AT new highs (0% off), RSI 52, 12.7x, with the group's second-strongest TV rating (STRONG BUY, 16/1). A high-yield anchor for a risk-off tape.

picks.name: Crocs, Inc.
picks.thesis: The cheapest breakout in the scan at 9.3x forward — Crocs is a VCP 2.5% off highs with RSI 63. A value-momentum combination in consumer discretionary that TV confirms BUY (14/5).
strengths: VCP near highs, RS at high
watch: Hold above the pivot into the print

picks.name: W.W. Grainger, Inc.
picks.thesis: Industrial-distribution compounder in a VCP near highs with rev +10% and EPS +18%. High-quality, steady-grower leadership in the rotating industrials group. TV confirms BUY (11/5).
strengths: VCP + RS at high, durable margins

picks.name: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc.
picks.thesis: Intermodal/transport leader breaking out with rev +19% and EPS +46% as the freight cycle turns. VCP + RS at high, earnings ~86 days out (clean runway). TV confirms strongly, BUY (15/1).
strengths: VCP + RS at high, rev +19%, EPS +46%

picks.name: Fortinet, Inc.
picks.thesis: The standout tech name while mega-cap semis correct — a tight VCP just 4% off highs with rev +20%, EPS +29% and a secular firewall-refresh tailwind. Cybersecurity is the defensive-growth pocket money is rotating into. TV confirms BUY (11/5). (Held position.)
strengths: Best-scoring tech name (94), VCP near highs
risks: 3-mo momentum +94% — extended run into earnings in ~9 days
watch: Volume on the push through prior highs

picks.name: Sirius XM Holdings Inc.
picks.thesis: Cheap (9x) cash-cow near highs (-2%) with RS at high and RSI 64 — a value/income breakout with defensive characteristics. TV confirms BUY (12/4). (Held position.)
strengths: 9x forward, RS at high, near highs
risks: Subscriber-growth stagnation is the long-term overhang
watch: Hold the pivot into the print

picks.name: Innovative Industrial Properties, Inc.
picks.thesis: Cannabis-focused net-lease REIT at new highs with RSI 68 and a cheap 13.4x multiple. High-yield income breakout confirmed by TV BUY (14/3) — but it's a niche, tenant-concentrated name, so size it accordingly.
strengths: At highs, VCP, cheap 13.4x, high yield

picks.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
picks.thesis: Energy-drink leader in a VCP 4.5% off highs with RSI 43 — the least-extended defensive-growth name here, with room to run and rev +27%. Note the tension: TV rates it only NEUTRAL (8 buy / 8 sell), so this is a fundamentals-led call awaiting a technical confirmation. (Held position.)
strengths: RSI 43 — lots of room, rev +27%, EPS +29%
risks: TV disagrees: NEUTRAL (8/8) — momentum not yet confirmed
watch: A push through the pivot to flip TV bullish

picks.name: Amazon.com, Inc.
picks.thesis: The mega-cap holding up best while the rest of big tech corrects — AMZN is only 9% off highs with RSI 63, rev +17%, EPS +75%, backed by AWS re-acceleration and retail margin gains. TV confirms BUY (14/2). (Held position.)
strengths: Relative strength vs the mega-cap group
risks: Would follow a broad mega-cap flush lower

picks.name: Okta, Inc.
picks.thesis: High-scoring identity-security name and TV BUY (14/3), but +96% three-month momentum makes it extended — a WATCH for a pullback or tight continuation base rather than a chase here.
strengths: Score 93, RS at high, secular identity/security demand
watch: A pullback to the 21-day / a tight flag

picks.name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
picks.thesis: LNG exporter with a strong TV rating (STRONG BUY, 16/0) and a clean energy-demand tailwind, but it's 10.6% off highs and RSI 69 — still repairing. Watch for it to reclaim highs before committing.
risks: 10.6% off highs — not yet a fresh breakout
watch: Reclaim of prior highs on volume

picks.name: Snowflake Inc.
picks.thesis: Data-cloud leader near highs (-2%) with rev +33% and +82% three-month momentum — TV even rates it STRONG BUY (16/1). But the RSI-71, big-run profile is extended into a risk-off tape, so treat it as a WATCH/hold rather than a fresh add. (Held position.)
watch: A pullback to the 21-day for adds

picks.name: Liquidia Corporation
picks.thesis: Small-cap pulmonary-hypertension name with an explosive +105% three-month run on its Yutrepia launch — near highs (-5%) and TV BUY (12/7). High reward, high volatility; watch/size carefully. (Held position.)
risks: Small-cap biotech volatility; binary catalyst risk

picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.thesis: The AI bellwether is 14% off highs and consolidating as semis correct — fundamentals remain elite (rev +85%, EPS +215%) but TV has slipped to NEUTRAL (7/9). A WATCH: hold core, wait for a base and a reclaim of the 50-day before adding. (Held position.)
strengths: Only mildly extended; long-term AI leader
risks: TV disagrees: NEUTRAL (7/9) — momentum has stalled
watch: Earnings ~37 days out; hyperscaler capex signals

picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
picks.thesis: Explosive fundamentals (rev +85%, EPS +325%) but the chart is stretched — 34% off highs yet RSI 75, and TV rates it only NEUTRAL (10/8). A WATCH into a volatile, richly-valued tape. (Held position.)

picks.name: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
picks.thesis: High scan score on the financials rotation (rev +42%, EPS +92%), but 8% off highs and TV is NEUTRAL (10/8) — the base isn't finished. A WATCH: wait for it to reclaim highs like its peers before adding. (Held position.)
watch: Reclaim of the pivot on volume
